executive_summary: Cross-diagram incident affecting 1 topology domain(s) (app_db_topology). 4 alert event(s) detected. Primary suspected root cause: APP-LB-01 in app_db_topology. Immediate triage recommended to validate and restore service.

Impacted nodes: APP-LB-01, APP-01, APP-02, DB-MASTER
Retrieved known resolution: KB-KR-APP-LB-001-002 — Known Resolution: Application Load Balancer Backend Health Degradation (Root Cause Confirmed)

CE-001 (temporal_correlation, confidence=1.0): 4 alert(s) within a 11-minute window (t=0..11 min), consistent with a propagating fault.
CE-002 (alert_sequence, confidence=0.85): Alert type sequence matches propagation chain: cpu -> latency. This ordering is consistent with fault propagation from the initiating node.

CE-003 (topology_proximity, confidence=0.95): Alerted nodes (APP-LB-01, APP-01, APP-02, DB-MASTER) are topologically proximate (topology score 0.95), suggesting fault propagation through connected infrastructure.
CE-004 (propagation_hypothesis, confidence=0.975): Hypothesis: fault initiated at APP-LB-01 (alert: cpu_spike) and propagated to 1 downstream node(s) matching known propagation patterns.
Correlation reason: Propagation pattern observed: cpu -> latency
Correlation reason: Alerted nodes are directly adjacent (1-2 hops)
pre_checks: Confirm root-cause candidate APP-LB-01 is present in enterprise graph memory.
Validate impacted diagrams before any change: app_db_topology.
Confirm RCA source and alert timeline freshness before remediation.
Run read-only reachability checks across diagram boundaries first.
triage_steps: Focus initial investigation on node 'APP-LB-01' in app_db_topology.
Check application server error logs for connection-refused or timeout patterns.
Verify database connection pool utilisation and active session count.
Confirm load balancer backend health checks are passing.
Review recent application deployments or configuration changes.
validation_steps: Confirm load balancer backend pool shows all members as healthy after remediation.
Send a synthetic health-probe request and verify application response is within threshold.
Verify application error rates in APM/logging have returned to baseline.
Confirm connection-timeout alerts have cleared on the monitoring platform.
Confirm health of all impacted nodes: APP-LB-01, APP-01, APP-02, DB-MASTER.
remediation_steps: Drain the affected backend pool members gracefully before making any configuration change.
Investigate backend health probe failures — check application health endpoint response codes.
Verify backend server CPU, memory, and thread-pool saturation before re-adding to pool.
After configuration change, confirm backend pool health-check status returns healthy.
Verify no recent application deployment introduced a health-endpoint regression.
Document the change and update the CMDB record for the affected load balancer.
post_checks: Confirm load balancer backend pool shows all members as healthy after remediation.
Send a synthetic health-probe request and verify application response is within threshold.
Verify application error rates in APM/logging have returned to baseline.
Confirm connection-timeout alerts have cleared on the monitoring platform.
Confirm health of all impacted nodes: APP-LB-01, APP-01, APP-02, DB-MASTER.
do_not_execute_if: Do not execute changes if root cause APP-LB-01 is not confirmed by graph evidence.
Do not execute if a rollback owner and maintenance window are not approved.
Do not execute if alert timestamps are stale or no longer match the active incident.
Do not execute cross-domain changes without NOC and owning team approval.
rollback_or_safety_notes: Capture running configs on all affected devices before any change.
Coordinate maintenance windows across affected sites — changes here have cross-domain blast radius.
Confirm a rollback plan is in place for each affected diagram domain.
Notify NOC before modifying WAN or shared-services components.
Stage changes in the least-critical diagram first; validate before proceeding.
escalation_recommendation: Escalate to network engineering team if root-cause node cannot be restored within 30 minutes, or if the incident spans more than 3 topology domains. Notify on-call SRE if customer-facing services remain impacted.
